{"step_by_step_thinking": "The lecithin-to-sphingomyelin ratio (L/S ratio) >2 is a well-established indicator of fetal lung maturity. The positive shake test is also commonly used to assess fetal lung maturity. Increased phosphatidyl glycerol is another biomarker for fetal lung maturity. However, the blue cells in the Nile Blue test are not a standard indicator of fetal lung maturity. The Nile Blue test is typically used to assess fetal liver maturity.", "answer_choice": "D"}
